risks.title: tree-sitter binary incompatibility on Windows (node-gyp rebuild)
risks.mitigation: Use pre-built wasm bindings (tree-sitter-{lang}/bindings/web) — no native compile required. Fall back to regex silently if wasm load fails.

risks.title: AST scanner slower than regex for large files, breaking performance SLA
risks.mitigation: Phase 1 includes perf benchmarks on coderef-core itself. Gate Phase 2 on <2x slowdown per file.

risks.title: Accuracy regression on edge-case patterns currently handled by regex heuristics
risks.mitigation: Ground-truth fixture tests per language before shipping each phase. Regex stays as fallback.

risks.title: scanner-worker.ts worker path not updated, causing silent accuracy downgrade for parallel scans
risks.mitigation: Phase 1 gate explicitly includes worker path update (resolves IMP-CORE-078 at the same time).

decision_records.decision: Create per-language AST scanner modules in src/scanner/ast/ rather than modifying scanner.ts inline
decision_records.rationale: Isolates grammar-specific logic, enables independent testing, and avoids further bloating the already-large scanner.ts. scanner.ts calls into ast/ modules.

decision_records.decision: Use tree-sitter wasm bindings, not native node bindings
decision_records.rationale: Eliminates node-gyp build requirement on Windows. wasm path is slower by ~15% but removes CI/install friction entirely.

decision_records.decision: Phase 1 resolves IMP-CORE-078 (worker path accuracy) at no extra cost
decision_records.rationale: scanner-worker.ts TS path is already the bug location — updating it to AST is part of Phase 1 scope, not a separate workorder.
